name: Credit Versio
description_short: AI-powered DIY credit repair software that guides users through disputing all three bureaus using automated letter generation, at a fraction of full-service costs.
description_long:
Credit Versio is a Las Vegas-based DIY credit repair software platform founded in 2020 by David Michalek, formerly of Fortress Credit Pro, with content contributions from John Ulzheimer, a recognized industry authority who previously worked at FICO and Equifax. Unlike traditional credit repair companies that assign a team to handle disputes on your behalf, Credit Versio provides an AI-powered software platform that walks you through the dispute process yourself. The software itself is free; monthly fees cover a required 3-bureau credit monitoring subscription through either SmartCredit or IdentityIQ, which the platform uses to import your credit reports and scores via soft pull — no hard inquiry required.

The platform's AI engine analyzes your credit reports and identifies up to 20 types of negative items, then recommends targeted dispute strategies for each. Users can generate dispute letters for all three major credit bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — as well as letters to original creditors, collections validation requests, cease-contact letters, goodwill letters, and identity theft claims. The software tracks monthly progress, showing which items were deleted, how scores changed, and suggesting updated strategies if initial disputes were unsuccessful. Monthly report updates let users see the impact of their disputes in near real-time, with the platform providing pre-recorded video coaching from credit experts to supplement the AI guidance.

What distinguishes Credit Versio most clearly is its price point and target user. At $19.95–$29.95 per month with no setup fee and no long-term contract, it costs significantly less than full-service credit repair, which typically runs $50–$150 per month. For consumers who are comfortable managing their own paperwork — including printing and physically mailing dispute letters themselves — the software delivers a structured, data-driven process that would otherwise require either paying a full-service company or doing extensive independent research. The AI guidance and progress tracking add meaningful structure for users who want a step-by-step roadmap rather than a blank template.

The trade-offs are important to understand before signing up. Credit Versio has no BBB profile and is not BBB accredited, which limits independent validation of its business practices. There is no live human support — no credit specialists, coaches, or legal staff to escalate complex disputes. Users must print and mail all dispute letters themselves, adding postage and printing costs not reflected in the monthly fee. Cancellation has also been flagged by some users as more difficult than expected, and the money-back guarantee advertised by some third-party review sites remains unverified. For consumers who want full-service support or legal escalation, a company like Lexington Law or Credit Saint would be more appropriate. But for self-sufficient users who want affordable, AI-guided structure at a low monthly cost, Credit Versio delivers strong value.

pricing.guarantee_details: No confirmed money-back guarantee. One third-party source reports a 90-day guarantee, but this is unverified and contradicted by other sources stating neither Credit Versio nor its monitoring partners offer refunds. Confirm directly with Credit Versio at support@creditversio.com before relying on any guarantee.

tiers.name: SmartCredit Basic
features: AI-powered dispute letter generation for all three bureaus
2 monthly single-bureau credit report updates via SmartCredit
Monthly progress tracking with score change reporting
Pre-recorded video coaching from credit experts

tiers.name: SmartCredit Premium
features: Identity theft insurance up to $1 million via SmartCredit
Faster dispute cycle tracking with more frequent report pulls

tiers.name: IdentityIQ Plan
features: AI-powered dispute letter generation for all three bureaus
Identity theft insurance up to $25,000 via IdentityIQ
Unlimited disputes with no monthly cap

pros: Significantly more affordable than full-service credit repair at $19.95–$29.95 per month
AI engine identifies up to 20 types of negative items and recommends dispute strategies
No setup fee and no long-term contract — cancel anytime
Generates letters for all three bureaus plus creditors, collectors, goodwill, and identity theft claims
Monthly progress tracking shows score changes and item deletions
No hard inquiry — imports reports via soft pull through monitoring partner
Content developed in part by John Ulzheimer, recognized ex-FICO and Equifax authority
cons: Users must print and mail all dispute letters themselves — no automatic submission
No live human support, credit specialists, or legal escalation available
No BBB profile and not BBB accredited, limiting independent trust verification
Cancellation process reported as more difficult than expected by some users
No dedicated mobile app — web portal only
best_for: Self-sufficient consumers comfortable managing their own paperwork and mailing
Budget-conscious users who want AI-guided structure without full-service pricing
People with multiple negative items across all three bureaus needing a systematic approach
Consumers who have been quoted high monthly fees by full-service credit repair companies
diagnosis: Best for self-motivated consumers who can handle printing and mailing letters themselves. Strong AI guidance at low cost, but no live support or BBB accountability — verify guarantee terms directly before subscribing.
services: AI-powered credit report analysis and dispute strategy recommendations
Dispute letter generation for all three credit bureaus
Monthly 3-bureau credit reports and score tracking via SmartCredit or IdentityIQ
Pre-recorded video coaching from credit industry experts
typical_results_timeline: Some users report score improvements of 60–100+ points within 1–3 months, though results vary widely depending on the number and complexity of negative items. Each dispute round takes approximately 30–45 days for bureaus to respond, with meaningful progress typically visible within 3–6 months for files with multiple derogatory items.
